title: woof critique front-matter
description: YAML front-matter of epics/E<N>/critique/<artefact>.md within the project state root in the operator home, ~/.woof/state/projects/<project-key>/ (ADR-017). Used at planning critique/plan.md and work-unit critique/work-unit-<id>.md. Top-level severity drives the gate-asymmetry rule: plan-gate always opens; work-unit gates open when severity=blocker. Findings list is advisory and unconstrained in count.

target.description: What was critiqued. plan = critique/plan.md. work_unit = critique/work-unit-<id>.md.

target_id.description: Work-unit ID when target=work_unit; null when target=plan. Coupled to target via allOf below.

severity.description: Roll-up severity over all findings. info = no concerns; minor = improvements but proceed; blocker = halt and review. Stage 5 Check 6 fails when this equals blocker.

harness.description: Identifier of the agent that produced the critique, e.g. 'codex-gpt-5.4', 'claude-opus-4-7'. Used for audit linkage.

session_ref.description: Audit pointer to the agent's session: CC session UUID for claude harnesses, audit-file path under the epic's audit/ directory in the project state root for codex. Optional but recommended.

findings.description: Individual findings the critique surfaced. Empty array allowed when severity=info.

id.description: Finding ID for cross-reference (F1, F2, ...). Sequential within the critique document.

category.description: Critique category. Stage 3 focus areas (outcome_coverage, decomposition, scope_hygiene, dependency_correctness, contract_implementation, class_2_architectural) plus Stage 5 areas (marker_semantic_mismatch, test_quality). Optional; absent => other.

summary.description: One-sentence prose statement of the finding.

evidence.description: Resolvable artefact evidence for the finding. Required (and non-empty) when severity=blocker; optional for non-blocker severities. Must cite at least one explicit reference: file:line (tracked path), a work-unit id present in the plan, O<n> (outcome id), CD<n> (contract-decision id), schemas/*.schema.json (schema path), or gate:<name> (quality-gate with explicit prefix). Bare prose and bare gate names do not resolve.

suggestion.description: Prose suggested resolution. Optional; advisory only.
